CLAUDE.md

Source: CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

Commands

Load the package:

uncovr::reload()

Run the test suite:

uncovr::test()

Run a single test file:

uncovr::test(filter = "r-session")

Regenerate documentation (man/ files):

uncovr::document()

Run full package check:

withr::with_envvar(c(NOT_CRAN = "true", DISPLAY = NA), rcmdcheck::rcmdcheck())

Architecture

callr lets R code call R functions in separate R processes. There are three execution models and three command types:

Execution models: - Synchronous one-off (r(), rcmd(), rscript()): spawns a process, waits for completion, returns result - Asynchronous one-off (r_bg(), rcmd_bg()): spawns a process, returns an R6 object immediately; caller polls/waits - Persistent session (r_session$new()): a reusable R process that accepts multiple sequential function calls over its lifetime

Command types: - R function evaluation — r(), r_bg(), r_session: serialize a closure to RDS, run it in a subprocess, deserialize the result - R CMD commands — rcmd(), rcmd_bg(): run R CMD <subcommand> with arguments - R scripts — rscript(): run a .R file via Rscript

Key source files:

File Role
R/eval.R r() — main synchronous entry point
R/eval-bg.R r_bg() — async entry point
R/r-process.R r_process R6 class (extends processx::process) used by r_bg()
R/r-session.R r_session R6 class — persistent subprocess with a message loop
R/rcmd.R / R/rcmd-bg.R rcmd() / rcmd_bg() entry points
R/script.R Generates the R script that runs inside the subprocess
R/setup.R Serializes the function + args to a temp RDS file for transport
R/result.R Deserializes the result RDS written by the subprocess
R/error.R Error classes (callr_error, callr_timeout_error, callr_status_error) and propagation
R/options.R Builds the option lists consumed by r_process, rcmd_process, rscript_process
R/hook.R Hook system run inside the subprocess before user code (library paths, repos, etc.)
R/package.R Package init, loads the processx C client library (client.so/.dll)

Subprocess communication: The parent process writes a function + arguments to a temp .rds file; the generated script (from script.R) reads and evaluates it, then writes the return value (or error) to another .rds file. r_session uses a persistent connection via the processx C client for lower-overhead repeated calls.

Safety defaults: By default, r() and friends run with --no-save --no-restore, no user profile, and a forced CRAN mirror — see r_safe() in R/presets.R.

Testing

Tests live in tests/testthat/. Snapshot files are in tests/testthat/_snaps/. A small test package (csomag/) lives in tests/testthat/fixtures/ and is used for library-path and installation tests.

Many tests are skipped on CRAN (skip_on_cran()). The NOT_CRAN environment variable controls this during local development.

On this page

Developed by Gábor Csárdi, Winston Chang, , Ascent Digital Services.
